name: Cyber Resilience Act
description: EU Cyber Resilience Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/2847) — horizontal cybersecurity requirements for products with digital elements placed on the EU market. Imposes secure-by-design, vulnerability disclosure, software supply chain, and CE-marking obligations on manufacturers.
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud-deployed software with digital elements in scope; affects SaaS and many cloud-native products.
relationToAISecurity: AI systems with digital elements fall under CRA; intersects with AI Act for high-risk AI.

license.notes: EU legislation published in OJEU. Reuse permitted under EUR-Lex terms; attribution expected.

lifecycle.notes: Entered into force 2024-12-10; main obligations apply from 2027-12-11.

current_extraction.tool: Playwright (HTML retrieval, EUR-Lex WAF bypass) + extract_eu_regulation.py (ELI parser)
current_extraction.notes: HTML fetched via Playwright (EUR-Lex WAF blocks automated curl) then parsed with the generic regulation/europa.eu/extract_eu_regulation.py using ELI class markup. CELEX 32024R2847. Output: CRA-articles.{csv,json} (71 articles), CRA-recitals.{csv,json} (130 recitals), CRA-annexes.{csv,json} (8 annexes).

extraction_history.reason: Initial structured extraction; HTML fetched via Playwright (EUR-Lex WAF) and parsed via shared ELI extractor

notes: Metadata stub; acquire source and extract.
